IsA: It is a particular type of icon of the Theotokos.
It is a type of icon of the Virgin Mary.
It is a reference to the prophecy of Isaiah 7:14.
It is a depiction of the Theotokos during the Annunciation.
It is a depiction of the Christ child at the moment of his conception.
HasA: It has her hands raised in the orans position.
It has the image of the Child Jesus depicted within a round aureole upon her breast.
It has the image of the Christ child representing him at the moment of his conception.
It has the Christ child vested in divine robes.
It has the Christ child holding a scroll.
It has the Christ child's face depicted as that of an old man.
It has the Christ child's right hand raised in blessing.
It has the letters ΜΡ ΘΥ on the upper left and right of the head of the Virgin Mary.
It has the image of "Emmanuel" on an oval shield.
It has a 9-foot-wide Iconic Monstrance.

It is characterized by being depicted either full length or half.

It is characterized by the image of "Emmanuel" on an oval shield.
It is characterized by being on a dark blue background, often adorned by golden stars.
UsedFor: It is used for representing the Theotokos during the Annunciation.
It is used for representing the Christ child at the moment of his conception.
It is used for symbolizing divine glory.
It is used for symbolizing the two natures of Christ.
It is used for indicating the Christian teaching that he was at one and the same time both a fully human infant and fully the eternal God.
It is used for reference to the prophecy of Isaiah 7:14.
It is used for placement in the apse of the sanctuary of an Orthodox church above the Holy Table.
It is used for poetically describing Mary as more spacious than the Heavens.
It is used for veneration.
It is used for private meditation and contemplation.
LocatedIn: It is located in the apse of the sanctuary of an Orthodox church.
It is located above the Holy Table.
It is located on the half-dome that stands above the altar.
It is located high above the iconostasis.
It is located facing down the length of the nave of the church.
It is located in Rome.
It is located in Russia.
It is located in the Novgorod Republic.
It is located in Chicago.
It is located in the Church of St. Stanislaus Kostka.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 4th century.
It was created in the 6th century.
It was created in the 11th to 12th centuries.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the Theotokos facing the viewer directly.
It consists of her hands raised in the orans position.
It consists of the image of the Child Jesus depicted within a round aureole upon her breast.
It consists of the Christ child vested in divine robes.
It consists of the Christ child holding a scroll.
It consists of the Christ child's face depicted as that of an old man.
It consists of the Christ child's right hand raised in blessing.
It consists of the letters ΜΡ ΘΥ on the upper left and right of the head of the Virgin Mary.
It consists of the image of "Emmanuel" on an oval shield.
It consists of a dark blue background, often adorned by golden stars.
PartOf: It is part of Christian art.
It is part of Orthodox church iconography.
It is part of the planned Sanctuary of The Divine Mercy.
It is part of the sanctuary's adoration chapel.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Theotokos.
